expectations.text: Explains that shadow size/softness communicates elevation — closer elements have larger, softer shadows
expectations.evidence: 'From Refactoring UI: closer = more prominent = larger, softer shadow...Shadows don't just add decoration — they convey how high an element floats above the page surface'

expectations.text: Justifies why the modal should have a larger/heavier shadow than the tooltip based on elevation
expectations.evidence: 'Modals sit at the top of the z-layer stack...represent the highest-elevation element in most UIs'; 'Tooltips are closer in character to dropdowns and popovers — small, contextual, lightweight'

expectations.text: Includes specific CSS box-shadow values for at least one element
expectations.evidence: Modal: `0 20px 25px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0 10px 10px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.04)`; Tooltip: `0 4px 6px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.07), 0 2px 4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.06)` — both provided

expectations.text: Recommends building a reusable shadow scale/system rather than ad-hoc values
expectations.evidence: 'Build a Named Elevation System...define a small set of named levels: --shadow-sm, --shadow-md, --shadow-lg, --shadow-xl, --shadow-2xl' with complete CSS custom properties

eval_feedback.overall: All four assertions pass. The two-part shadow (ambient + direct) advice goes beyond the assertions and represents genuine skill-added value.
